description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is marked with alternating yellow and black vertical stripes, which are weathered and show signs of wear, likely from prolonged outdoor exposure. The barrier has a trapezoidal cross-section, with a broad, stable base and a slightly narrower top, designed to deflect vehicles and prevent crossing. The concrete is visibly rough and stained, with some areas showing minor chipping and surface erosion. The painted stripes are uneven and faded, with streaks and drips indicating exposure to rain and grime. There are no visible reflective strips, but the high-contrast paint serves as a visual warning.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with faded yellow and black stripes, showing signs of outdoor wear and minor chipping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barrier with prominent yellow and black vertical stripes and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the trapezoidal profile and thickness of the barrier, with stripes visible along the sloped surface.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same striped pattern and weathered concrete texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the sloped profile and the continuation of the painted stripes.
